channel.title: Repository on Pi Stack
channel.description: Recent content in Repository on Pi Stack

item.title: BaGet vs NuGet.Server vs SlimGet: Self-Hosted NuGet Package Servers 2026
item.description: For .NET developers, NuGet is the standard package management system — the equivalent of npm for JavaScript or pip for Python. While the public nuget.org repository hosts hundreds of thousands of packages, organizations that build internal libraries, need offline access, or want to control exactly which packages reach their production systems require a self-hosted NuGet server.

item.title: Aptly vs Reprepro vs Apt-mirror: Self-Hosted Debian Package Repository Management 2026
item.description: Managing Debian and Ubuntu packages across multiple servers requires a reliable, self-hosted package repository. Whether you need to distribute custom-built .deb files, mirror upstream repositories for offline access, or maintain a stable snapshot of package versions for reproducible deployments, a self-hosted apt repository gives you full control over your software supply chain.
